angle_summary: Woodfire Westport has one of the strongest review profiles in its local market, but the website currently has no booking system or contact form to convert that reputation into direct reservations. A focused conversion sprint would give diners a clear pathway from discovery to confirmed table, capturing commercial value that is currently being left on the table.
proof_points: 573 Google reviews at 4.6 stars represent a significant trust asset that is not currently surfaced on the website, meaning visitors arriving directly to the site see no social proof at the point of decision.
No booking system and no contact form are present on the site, leaving visitors with high dining intent and no on-site action to take beyond browsing.

Mobile PageSpeed score of 74 on a WordPress site with a desktop score of 99 confirms that mobile-specific improvements are achievable without a full rebuild, directly benefiting the majority of restaurant discovery searches.
offer_scope_points: Install and configure a reservation or enquiry widget, such as OpenTable, ResDiary, or a lightweight contact form, providing a direct on-site booking pathway.
Embed a curated Google reviews section or live review widget on the homepage to surface the 4.6-star rating and 573 reviews at the point of visitor decision-making.
Audit and implement a prominent click-to-call phone number above the fold on mobile, ensuring every visitor has an immediate low-friction action available.
personalisation_notes: With 573 Google reviews at 4.6 stars, Woodfire Westport has one of the most substantial review profiles for a restaurant in Co. Mayo, making this a highly specific and credible hook for outreach.

The site is built on WordPress with a desktop PageSpeed of 99, which means all proposed conversion changes can be implemented within the existing platform without disruption to what is already working well.
suggested_cta: Would you be open to a 15-minute call this week so I can walk you through exactly what these changes would look like and how quickly they could be in place?
avoid_saying: Avoid suggesting the current website is failing or underperforming, as the business is clearly busy and may feel the site is adequate given existing phone and walk-in trade.

Avoid implying the business needs a full website redesign or platform change, as the audit confirms the technical foundation is strong and the opportunity is specifically about adding conversion functionality.
unknowns_to_confirm: Whether the business currently accepts reservations or operates as walk-in only, as this determines whether a booking widget or a simple enquiry form is the more appropriate and sellable solution.
Whether a phone number is currently displayed on the site and whether it is click-to-call enabled on mobile, as this shapes the urgency and scope of the mobile conversion recommendation.
